era_context: The late 12th century CE was a period of significant political and military realignments across Eurasia. The Crusader states were under increasing pressure from Saladin's consolidating power. In Europe, feudal monarchies were strengthening; in Asia, the Jin Dynasty controlled northern China, the Song Dynasty the south, and the Khmer Empire was at its height. Japan was in the midst of the Genpei War.

events.title: Genpei War continues with Minamoto advances
events.description: The Genpei War (1180–1185) continued in 1182 with significant Minamoto victories. The Minamoto, led by Minamoto no Yoritomo and his brother Yoshitsune, continued to gain ground against the Taira clan. The Minamoto were consolidating their control over eastern Japan and preparing for the final push against the Taira in the west. By 1182, the outcome of the war was becoming increasingly clear, with the Minamoto in the ascendant.

sources.name: Genpei Seisui Ki (Record of the Rise and Fall of the Minamoto and Taira)

events.certainty_note: The Genpei War's progression is well-documented in Japanese historical records. The year 1182 falls within the period of Minamoto consolidation.

events.title: Saladin consolidates power in Egypt and the Levant
events.description: By 1182, Saladin had largely consolidated his control over Egypt and the Levant, unifying the Muslim Near East under a single ruler for the first time in decades. His administrative reforms and military reorganization during this period laid the groundwork for his subsequent campaigns against the Crusader states. Saladin was systematically reducing Crusader strongholds and preparing for the decisive campaigns of 1187.
key_figures: Saladin (Ṣalāḥ ad-Dīn Yūsuf ibn Ayyūb, Sultan of Egypt and Syria)

sources.name: Baha ad-Din Ibn Shaddad, Life of Saladin
Imad ad-Din al-Isfahani, Conquest of Jerusalem

events.certainty_note: Saladin's consolidation of power is well-documented in Islamic sources. The process was largely complete by 1182.

events.title: Frederick Barbarossa negotiates with the Lombard League
events.description: Emperor Frederick I Barbarossa continued negotiations with the Lombard League of northern Italian cities in 1182. These negotiations would eventually lead to the Peace of Constance in 1183. The negotiations reflected the growing power of urban centers in Italy and the declining ability of the Holy Roman Emperor to impose direct control over Italian territories.
events.certainty_note: The negotiations between Frederick and the Lombard League are documented in contemporary sources, though the specific events of 1182 are less precisely dated than the final Peace of Constance in 1183.

events.title: Jayavarman VII continues expansion of the Khmer Empire
events.description: Jayavarman VII continued his reign as the most expansionist Khmer ruler, consolidating control over newly conquered territories and undertaking massive construction projects. By 1182, the Khmer Empire had reached its greatest territorial extent. His administrative reforms and building projects represented the height of Khmer power and cultural achievement.
events.certainty_note: Jayavarman VII's reign (1181–1220) is well-documented through inscriptions and archaeology. The specific events of 1182 are less precisely dated.

events.title: Jin Dynasty consolidates control over northern China
events.description: The Jin Dynasty (1115–1234), established by the Jurchen people, continued to consolidate its control over northern China during the reign of Emperor Shizong (r. 1161–1189). By 1182, the Jin Dynasty had established a stable administrative system and was at the height of its power. The dynasty maintained a tense coexistence with the Song Dynasty in the south.

sources.name: Jin Shi (History of the Jin Dynasty)
Song Shi (History of the Song Dynasty)

events.certainty_note: The Jin Dynasty's control over northern China is well-documented in Chinese historical records. Emperor Shizong's reign (1161–1189) is securely dated.

disconfirming_evidence: The specific events of 1182 are less precisely documented than those of surrounding years. The Genpei War's progression is well-documented, but the exact dating of individual campaigns in 1182 is less certain. The negotiations between Frederick Barbarossa and the Lombard League are documented, but the specific events of 1182 are less clearly delineated than the final Peace of Constance in 1183.
historiographic_note: The historical record for 1182 is dominated by sources from Japan, the Islamic world, and Europe. Asian sources (Khmer inscriptions, Chinese chronicles) are less frequently synthesized in Western historiography. Sub-Saharan Africa, Eastern Europe, and the Americas are poorly represented. The sources reflect the interests of literate elites and may underrepresent social, economic, and cultural developments. The year 1182 is less densely documented than surrounding years, suggesting that major events may have been overlooked or that this was a year of consolidation rather than dramatic change.

graph_edges.note: Minamoto consolidation in 1182 led to further advances in 1183
Saladin's consolidation in 1182 led to further military victories in 1183
Negotiations in 1182 led to the Peace of Constance in 1183
